project_specification.project_goal: Render the complete CauseFlow product and API documentation site from MDX through Mintlify, with every page reachable from navigation, zero broken links, valid frontmatter, and machine-verifiable content invariants holding across all 133 `.mdx` files.
project_specification.overview:
The CauseFlow public documentation is a Mintlify-hosted MDX site that documents the CauseFlow AI product (incident investigation, integrations, billing, security), its REST API, and the CauseFlow Relay privacy-preserving database agent. It is the single source of truth that customers and integrators consult before integrating with CauseFlow or evaluating its plan tiers.
The site is built from MDX files organized under `getting-started/`, `dashboard/`, `integrations/`, `billing/`, `security/`, `api-reference/`, `relay/`, and `changelog/`. Site structure, navigation, colors, logo, and redirects live in `docs.json`; navigation is grouped into four tabs (Documentation, API reference, Relay, Changelog). Content invariants in `INVARIANTS.md` enforce vocabulary and boundary rules (severity enum, status enum, API host, no internal AWS identifiers, approved placeholder formats) that are checked by a `check-invariants.sh` PostToolUse hook.

technology_stack.general: Mintlify documentation platform; MDX (Markdown + JSX) page format; Mintlify CLI (`mint`) for local preview and broken-link checks; Mermaid for inline diagrams; Mintlify components for layout (`Card`, `CardGroup`, `Steps`, `Note`, `Tip`, `Warning`, `Info`, `CodeGroup`, `ParamField`, `Tabs`); Mintlify GitHub App publishes the site automatically on push to the default branch; the repo holds only source content — no application code, no build server, no test runner beyond Mintlify's own validators.

frontend.styling: Mintlify theme tokens (primary `#1A8B6F`, light `#4ECDC4`, dark `#26233E`); no custom CSS
frontend.routing: Mintlify file-based routing driven by `docs.json` navigation; explicit page paths per group
frontend.port: Local dev server runs on port 3000 via `mint dev`
frontend.patterns: MDX + Mintlify components over custom JSX; reusable snippets under `snippets/`; no client-side state, no SPA shell

technology_stack.communication: removed (the docs site is read-only content; the API being documented uses REST + SSE; Relay uses WSS — those are out of scope for this project)

integration.name: Mintlify GitHub App
integration.responsibility: Auto-builds and publishes the site when commits land on the default branch. The repo at `causeflow/public-docs` is connected to a Mintlify deployment; pushes to the default branch trigger a build and deploy to `docs.causeflow.ai`.
integration.configuration: Configured externally in the Mintlify dashboard — points at the GitHub repo and default branch. No in-repo credentials, no `.env` keys, no webhook secrets in this repository.
integration.security: The Mintlify GitHub App receives a GitHub App installation scoped to the repo; build artifacts are served from Mintlify-managed infrastructure. This repo holds no long-lived secrets.
integration.failure_behavior: If the GitHub App is uninstalled, pushes do not deploy — the site continues to serve the last successful build until the App is reinstalled or builds are restored.
integration.deployment: Production deployment is fully delegated to the Mintlify GitHub App; no CI workflow in this repo handles production deploys. Local replacement: `mint dev` for preview, `mint broken-links` to validate.
integration.affected_surfaces: Every page in the site — all 133 MDX files, `docs.json`, logo and favicon assets.

integration.name: Mintlify contextual AI
integration.responsibility: Provides the AI assistant menu in the docs reader — readers can copy a page, view source, or open the page in Claude or ChatGPT for follow-up questions. Surfaced as a per-page "contextual" menu in Mintlify.
integration.configuration: Selected in `docs.json` under `contextual.options`. No credentials in this repo — the menu delegates to Mintlify-managed providers.
integration.security: External AI providers (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI ChatGPT) receive page content only when a reader explicitly invokes the menu item. No credentials held in this repo; the page payload is the only data crossing the boundary.
integration.failure_behavior: If a provider is unavailable, that menu option silently fails for the reader; the rest of the site renders normally.
integration.deployment: Managed by Mintlify; toggled by editing `docs.json`.
integration.affected_surfaces: Reader chrome on every page; not visible in MDX source content.

prerequisites.environment_setup:
Node.js (any active LTS) is required to install and run the Mintlify CLI. The CLI is installed globally with `npm i -g mint`. The local development preview is `mint dev` run from the project root (the directory that contains `docs.json`); the dev server listens on `http://localhost:3000`. Link validation is `mint broken-links` and must return zero broken links.
This repository holds no `.env` file and no secrets: authentication, publishing, and the contextual AI menu are all delegated to the Mintlify GitHub App and Mintlify-managed providers. The `skills-lock.json` file pins the Mintlify skill version. `INVARIANTS.md` defines the content invariants enforced by the `check-invariants.sh` PostToolUse hook.

core_features.foundation: - Mintlify dev server boots from this directory and serves `http://localhost:3000` without errors - `mint broken-links` exits 0 - `docs.json` validates against the Mintlify schema (`https://mintlify.com/docs.json`) - The Mintlify GitHub App is the only publishing path; no CI deploy workflow in this repo - README documents the local preview and broken-link commands
core_features.content-structure: - Top-level directory layout: `getting-started/`, `dashboard/`, `integrations/`, `billing/`, `security/`, `api-reference/`, `relay/`, `changelog/`, `snippets/`, `investigation/`, `plans/`, `tasks/`, `docs/`, `logo/` - Every `.mdx` has YAML frontmatter with `title` and `description`; `description` is at most 160 characters - `docs.json` declares four navigation tabs: Documentation, API reference, Relay, Changelog — every page listed under a group resolves to a real `.mdx` file - Snippets under `snippets/` are referenced by other pages rather than duplicated - `.mintignore` excludes `drafts/` and `*.draft.mdx` from build
core_features.product-narrative: - Getting-started tab covers Quickstart, key concepts, how-it-works, AI transparency, skills, memory-and-chat, and triggers - Dashboard tab covers overview, incidents, analyses, remediations, audit-trail, team-management, settings - Integrations tab covers cloud providers, monitoring, GitHub, communication, project management, databases, custom webhooks, HubSpot - Billing tab covers plans, usage and credits, manage subscription - Security tab covers overview, authentication, RBAC, data privacy, compliance - All inbound cross-references between pages resolve at build time (no broken links)
core_features.api-reference: - Single base URL `https://api.causeflow.ai` and version prefix `/v1`; no divergent hostnames (`api.causeflow.io|dev|local|prod`) in any example - Authentication page documents JWT Bearer (header `Authorization: Bearer `) with Clerk-issued tokens, API keys (header `X-API-Key: cflo_…`), and webhook HMAC-SHA256 signatures (header `X-Webhook-Signature: sha256=…`) - Errors and pagination page documents the JSON error envelope, HTTP status codes (400/401/403/404/409/429/500/503), and cursor-based pagination - Outbound events catalog at `api-reference/webhooks/outbound-events.mdx` lists exactly 20 dot-namespaced EventBus events across seven domains (tenant, user, incident, investigation, remediation, integration, trigger, knowledge) - API examples use only the approved placeholder formats: `ten_EXAMPLE_…`, `cflo_live_sk_EXAMPLE_…`, truncated JWTs starting `eyJhbGc…`
core_features.relay-documentation: - Relay tab covers overview, architecture, quickstart, configuration, PII masking, policy engine, deployment, troubleshooting - `relay/overview.mdx` renders a Mermaid diagram of the outbound-only WSS flow - `relay/configuration.mdx` documents `relay-config.yaml` end-to-end (controlPlane, resources, operations, row limits, env-var substitution) - Relay section describes privacy guarantees: zero inbound traffic, read-only access, PII masking inside the customer network, audit trail, container hardening (non-root, read-only FS, no-new-privileges) - Relay API endpoints under `api-reference/relay/` align with the configuration reference
core_features.invariants-and-validation: - Severity vocabulary across all `.mdx` is restricted to `critical | high | medium | low | info` — no `emergency | urgent | notice | debug | warn` - Incident `status` vocabulary is restricted to the lifecycle `open → triaging → investigating → awaiting_approval → remediating → resolved → closed`; `pending` is allowed for step-level and approval-level status only - RBAC role vocabulary is `admin | member` only - No MDX references AWS ARNs, 12-digit account IDs adjacent to "account" or "arn", `.internal` hostnames, `sqs..amazonaws.com` URLs, KMS key IDs, LangFuse URLs, Hindsight URLs, internal DynamoDB table names, or ECS cluster/task-definition names - Tenant ID, API key, and JWT placeholders follow the approved formats only

acceptance_check.description: Running `mint dev` from the project root starts a local server and `GET http://localhost:3000/` returns HTTP 200 with HTML containing the site name "CauseFlow AI" and the intro card "Quickstart" — observed via curl or browser.
Running `mint broken-links` from the project root exits with status code 0 and reports zero broken links across the site.

`docs.json` is valid JSON and validates against the `https://mintlify.com/docs.json` JSON schema — every navigation `tab` declares its `groups` and every `pages` entry resolves to a real `.mdx` file under the project root.

Every `.mdx` file under the project root (excluding `.mintlify/`, `.artifacts/`, `node_modules/`, `drafts/`) has YAML frontmatter with both `title` and `description` fields. A scripted find + head -5 + grep returns no `MISSING TITLE: …` lines.
Every `description` field in `.mdx` frontmatter is at most 160 characters — `awk` over frontmatter blocks returns no field whose value length exceeds 160.

All four navigation tabs declared in `docs.json` (Documentation, API reference, Relay, Changelog) render in the local Mintlify dev server; clicking the Changelog tab reaches a page whose H1 matches the title in `changelog/index.mdx`.

The redirects block of `docs.json` (currently `{"source": "/quickstart", "destination": "/getting-started/quickstart"}`) resolves at runtime — `GET /quickstart` returns 200 and lands on the Quickstart page.

Each of the seven pages listed under Getting started (Quickstart, Key concepts, How it works, AI transparency, Skills, Memory and chat, Triggers) is reachable from the documentation tab in `mint dev` and renders without MDX parse errors.
Each of the seven Dashboard pages (Overview, Incidents, Analyses, Remediations, Audit trail, Team management, Settings) is reachable from the documentation tab and renders.
Each of the nine Integrations pages (Overview, Monitoring, GitHub, Communication, Project management, Databases, Custom webhooks, Cloud providers, HubSpot) is reachable and renders.

Each of the three Billing pages (Plans, Usage and credits, Manage subscription), the five Security pages (Overview, Authentication, RBAC, Data privacy, Compliance), and the Changelog index is reachable and renders.

The API reference introduction page renders with H1 "API introduction", the base-URL code block showing exactly `https://api.causeflow.ai`, and the version note "v1" — `mint dev` does not log a parse error for `api-reference/introduction.mdx`.

Every endpoint page listed under the API reference tab in `docs.json` (incidents, triage, investigation, remediation, memory, skills, triggers, integrations, knowledge, graph, billing, notifications, audit, webhooks, GitHub, tenants, API keys, analytics, health, widget, relay) renders its H1 matching the `title` frontmatter — observed by curling each rendered URL in `mint dev`.

The Authentication page (`api-reference/authentication.mdx`) renders sections for JWT Bearer tokens, API keys, and Webhook HMAC signature verification, each with a working code example; the `verifyWebhookSignature` snippet compiles syntactically (Node.js `node --check`).

The Errors and pagination page renders the JSON error envelope example, the HTTP status-code table (400/401/403/404/409/429/500/503), and a cursor-pagination example with `items`, `cursor`, and `count` fields.
Every API example URL across the api-reference pages uses the host `https://api.causeflow.ai` only — `grep -rE 'api\.causeflow\.(io|dev|local|prod)' --include='*.mdx'` returns no matches.

Every tenant-ID placeholder in api-reference examples matches the `ten_EXAMPLE_…` shape and every API-key placeholder matches `cflo_live_sk_EXAMPLE_…` — no real-looking UUIDs or full JWTs appear in any example (`grep -rE '(ten_[a-zA-Z0-9]{16,})|(cflo_live_sk_[a-zA-Z0-9]{16,})' --include='*.mdx' | grep -v EXAMPLE` returns nothing).

The Outbound events catalog (`api-reference/webhooks/outbound-events.mdx`) renders a table whose row count equals exactly 20 distinct dot-namespaced events, and the introduction page either states the same count or links to this page as the source of truth — no off-by-one in the headline count. Event catalog lists 20 events; the introduction page's 21-event count is stale and the introduction must align to 20.

The Relay tab renders each of its eight pages (Overview, Architecture, Quickstart, Configuration, PII masking, Policy engine, Deployment, Troubleshooting) without MDX parse errors, and the Overview page renders the Mermaid `flowchart TD` block (Mintlify serves the rendered diagram, not raw code).

The Configuration page documents `controlPlane`, `resources`, `allowedOperations`, `maxRowsPerQuery`, and the `${VAR_NAME}` env-var substitution — observed by `grep` for each of those keys returning at least one match in `relay/configuration.mdx`.

The Relay overview's "What the Relay is not" section names proxy, tunnel, and replication-agent anti-patterns — `grep -E 'Not a (proxy|tunnel)|replication agent' relay/overview.mdx` returns all three matches.

Across every `.mdx`, the only severity tokens that appear are `critical | high | medium | low | info` — `grep -rEn 'severity[: ].*(emergency|urgent|notice|debug|warn)' --include='*.mdx'` returns no matches.

Across every `.mdx`, no `Incident.status` value of `"dismissed"` or `"failed"` appears; `"pending"` appears only for step-level or approval-level status — `grep -rEn '"status": *"(dismissed|failed)"' --include='*.mdx'` returns no matches.

No MDX references AWS ARNs, `.internal` hostnames, `sqs..amazonaws.com` URLs, 12-digit account IDs adjacent to "account" or "arn", KMS key IDs, LangFuse URLs, Hindsight URLs, internal DynamoDB table names, or ECS cluster/task-definition names — `grep -rEi '(arn:aws:|\.internal[^a-z]|sqs\.[a-z0-9-]+\.amazonaws\.com|[0-9]{12}.*(account|arn)|kms:key/[a-f0-9-]{36}|langfuse|hindsight\.[a-z]+|causeflow-(staging|production)[- ]|/ecs/causeflow)' --include='*.mdx'` returns no matches.

RBAC role names across all MDX are restricted to `admin` and `member`; no other role tokens appear in RBAC context — observed by `grep -rn 'roles\?:' --include='*.mdx'` and confirming only the two role names are listed under any `roles` or `role:` line.

project_specification.api_endpoints_summary:
The api-reference section documents the CauseFlow control-plane API at `https://api.causeflow.ai/v1/`. Endpoints are grouped by surface; each is one method on one path. The docs site does not implement any of these endpoints — it documents them.
Incidents — GET/POST /v1/incidents, GET/PATCH /v1/incidents/{id}, POST /v1/incidents/{id}/status Triage — POST /v1/incidents/{id}/triage, POST /v1/incidents/{id}/classify, GET /v1/incidents/{id}/evidence, GET /v1/incidents/{id}/evidence/list Investigation — POST /v1/investigations, POST /v1/investigations/{id}/context, GET /v1/investigations/{id}/results, GET /v1/investigations/{id}/known-solution, POST /v1/investigations/{id}/feedback, POST /v1/investigations/{id}/abort Remediation — GET /v1/remediations, POST /v1/remediations/{id}/approve, POST /v1/remediations/{id}/reject, POST /v1/remediations/{id}/execute, POST /v1/remediations/{id}/feedback, GET /v1/remediations/{id} Memory and Chat — POST /v1/memory/chat, GET /v1/memory/insights, GET /v1/memory/runbooks, GET /v1/memory/chat-history Skills — POST/GET /v1/skills, GET/PATCH/DELETE /v1/skills/{id} Triggers — GET/POST /v1/triggers, DELETE /v1/triggers/{id} Integrations — GET /v1/integrations, GET /v1/integrations/catalog, GET/POST /v1/integrations/credentials, POST /v1/integrations/connect Knowledge — GET /v1/knowledge/patterns, POST /v1/knowledge/search, POST /v1/knowledge/feedback Graph — POST /v1/graph/services, POST /v1/graph/dependencies, GET /v1/graph/blast-radius, POST /v1/graph/auto-discovery Billing — GET /v1/billing/plans, GET /v1/billing/subscription, GET /v1/billing/credits, GET /v1/billing/usage, POST /v1/billing/purchase Notifications — GET /v1/notifications/stream, GET /v1/notifications, POST /v1/notifications/{id}/read, GET /v1/notifications/approvals-pending, POST /v1/notifications/approvals/respond, GET /v1/notifications/list, POST /v1/notifications/respond Audit — GET /v1/audit, POST /v1/audit/verify, POST /v1/audit/export Webhooks — POST /v1/webhooks/alert, GET /v1/webhooks/events, POST /v1/webhooks/subscribe GitHub — GET /v1/github/installation, DELETE /v1/github/installation Tenants — GET/POST /v1/tenants, GET/PATCH /v1/tenants/{id} API keys — POST/GET /v1/api-keys, DELETE /v1/api-keys/{id} Analytics — GET /v1/analytics/incidents Health — GET /v1/health, GET /v1/health/detailed Widget (roadmap) — POST /v1/widget/sessions, POST /v1/widget/sessions/{id}/messages, GET /v1/widget/sessions/{id}/stream, POST /v1/widget/sessions/{id}/push, POST /v1/widget/sessions/{id}/close Relay — GET /v1/relay/status, POST /v1/relay/connect (control-plane side; the Relay's outbound WSS endpoint is `wss://api.causeflow.ai/v1/relay/connect`)
Real-time delivery today: Server-Sent Events on `GET /v1/notifications/stream`. Programmatic webhook subscription is on the roadmap.

ui_layout.main_structure: Static Mintlify site. Persistent chrome: top navbar with a Support mailto link and a primary "Dashboard" button pointing to `https://dashboard.causeflow.ai`; left sidebar with four tabs (Documentation, API reference, Relay, Changelog) and grouped pages; main content area. Global anchors in the top-right link to "Dashboard" and a "Status" health badge pointing at `https://api.causeflow.ai/v1/health`. The contextual AI menu (Copy / View / Claude / ChatGPT) appears on every page.
ui_layout.sidebar: Tabbed, one tab per `docs.json` `tab`; each tab holds a vertical list of `groups`, each group a heading and a list of page links. The active page is highlighted. Tabs collapse on narrow viewports.
ui_layout.main: Single column. Top of page: H1 (from frontmatter `title`) and a subtitle. Body: MDX content with components (``, ``, ``, ``, ``, ``, ``, ``, ``, ``). Inline Mermaid blocks are rendered as SVG.

design_system.color_palette: Primary `#1A8B6F` (CauseFlow teal), accent light `#4ECDC4`, dark text/background `#26233E`. Theme defaults to light appearance; switchable in Mintlify's reader. Logo is light/dark SVG under `/logo/light.svg` and `/logo/dark.svg`; favicon is `/favicon.svg`.
design_system.typography: Mintlify theme defaults; no custom font stack in this repo. Headings use sentence case (per `CLAUDE.md` style rules). UI elements are bolded; code, file names, commands, and paths use monospace.
design_system.components: Prefer built-in Mintlify components over custom JSX. Reusable snippets live under `snippets/` (currently `auth-header.mdx` and `rate-limit-note.mdx`) and are imported from other pages. Custom Markdown inside snippets is rendered as the snippet's content.
design_system.animations: Mintlify theme defaults — no custom animation code in this repo.

project_specification.key_interactions:
1. Reader lands on `docs.causeflow.ai` (homepage) and reads the How-it-works flow. 2. Reader clicks Quickstart, follows the seven-step account → profile → connect-tools → create-incident → review-results walkthrough, with side links to the API reference for programmatic flows. 3. Reader opens the API reference tab, lands on Introduction, then Authentication, then drills into a specific endpoint (for example, `POST /v1/incidents`). Each endpoint page renders path, method, headers, request body, response, and error codes; the right rail shows the table of contents for the current page. 4. Reader evaluates Relay: opens the Relay tab, reads Overview (including the Mermaid diagram), then Configuration to copy the `relay-config.yaml` shape with `${VAR_NAME}` placeholders, then Deployment to follow the Docker / Kubernetes / ECS Fargate steps. 5. Reader checks status by clicking the global "Status" anchor in the top-right; the link goes to `https://api.causeflow.ai/v1/health`. 6. Reader exports a page to Claude or ChatGPT via the contextual AI menu when a question needs more than the static doc.
For a contributor (writing/curating the site): 1. Editor runs `npm i -g mint` once. 2. Editor runs `mint dev` and edits `.mdx` files; the browser hot-reloads. 3. Editor runs `mint broken-links` before pushing; CI / the local PostToolUse `check-invariants.sh` enforces vocabulary and host invariants. 4. Editor pushes to the default branch; the Mintlify GitHub App builds and deploys to `docs.causeflow.ai`.

step.title: Foundation: Mintlify dev server, broken-link check, schema-valid docs.json
step.tasks: - Install Mintlify CLI: `npm i -g mint` (prereq, not committed to this repo). - From the project root, run `mint dev` and confirm `http://localhost:3000/` returns 200 with the home content (AC-001). - Run `mint broken-links` and confirm exit 0 and zero broken links (AC-002). - Validate `docs.json` against `https://mintlify.com/docs.json`; every navigation page resolves to a real `.mdx` (AC-003).

step.title: Content structure: frontmatter, navigation coverage, redirects
step.tasks: - Audit every `.mdx` for `title` + `description` frontmatter; fix any page missing either (AC-004). - Enforce `description` length ≤ 160 chars (AC-005). - Confirm all four tabs render end-to-end and the Changelog index H1 matches the frontmatter `title` (AC-006). - Confirm `/quickstart` redirect lands on the Quickstart page (AC-007).

step.title: Product narrative coverage: getting-started, dashboard, integrations, billing, security, changelog
step.tasks: - Walk all 7 Getting-started pages, all 7 Dashboard pages, all 9 Integrations pages, the 3 Billing + 5 Security + Changelog index — confirm reachability and no MDX parse errors (AC-008, AC-009, AC-010, AC-011).

step.title: API reference: introduction, authentication, errors/pagination, endpoint coverage, host and placeholder invariants
step.tasks: - Verify the introduction page renders with the canonical base URL and version (AC-012). - Verify every endpoint group listed in `docs.json` renders (AC-013). - Verify the Authentication page covers JWT, API key, and HMAC verification; HMAC example compiles (AC-014). - Verify Errors and pagination page renders the standard envelope, status table, and cursor example (AC-015). - Enforce host invariant (AC-016) and placeholder invariant (AC-017) across api-reference. - Reconcile the event count: outbound-events page lists 20; update api-reference/introduction.mdx to match the catalog (AC-018, resolves the documented contradiction).

step.title: Relay documentation: eight-page coverage, Mermaid diagram, configuration reference, anti-pattern callouts
step.tasks: - Verify all 8 Relay tab pages render and the Mermaid flowchart renders as SVG (AC-019). - Verify the Configuration page documents all required keys (AC-020). - Verify the "What the Relay is not" section names proxy/tunnel/replication-agent (AC-021).

step.title: Invariants and content boundaries
step.tasks: - Enforce the severity enum (AC-022), the incident status enum with the `pending` exception (AC-023), the AWS-identifier exclusion (AC-024), and the RBAC role enum (AC-025) via the `check-invariants.sh` PostToolUse hook in `.claude/settings.json` (already wired).

success_criteria.functionality: `mint dev` boots the site; every page in the four navigation tabs renders without MDX parse errors; `mint broken-links` returns 0; redirects in `docs.json` resolve; the contextual AI menu offers Copy / View / Claude / ChatGPT on every page.
success_criteria.user_experience: A first-time reader can move from the homepage to Quickstart to the API reference in three clicks; the Search bar (Mintlify) finds pages by title and body; the Mermaid diagram on the Relay Overview renders inline rather than as raw code.
success_criteria.technical_quality: Every `.mdx` has `title` and `description ≤ 160`; only the canonical API host appears; placeholder formats are limited to the approved shapes; the event count on the introduction page matches the catalog (20); content invariants are checked by the local hook and hold across all 133 MDX files.
success_criteria.design_polish: Mintlify theme tokens (primary `#1A8B6F`) are applied; logo and favicon resolve; sentence-case headings; bold UI element names; code formatting for file names, commands, paths, and code references; no custom CSS overrides.
